person.name: Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville

nameForms.fullText: Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville

memoryArtifacts.headline: geni.com
memoryArtifacts.description:
Not believed to have held lands in England. A supporter of Robert Curthose with his father, he was captured at St.Pierre-sur-Dive shortly before the battle of Tinchebrai.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville於大約1125年出生在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利瓦爾蒙特時，他的父親Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont26歲，母親Julienne de Thourotte26歲。他和Léonelle of Salisbury至少有5個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1185年之前在英國，埋葬在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利瓦爾蒙特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville于大约1125年出生在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利瓦爾蒙特时，他的父亲Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont26岁，母亲Julienne de Thourotte26岁。他和Léonelle of Salisbury至少有5个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1185年之前在英国，埋葬在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利瓦爾蒙特。

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville was born about 1125, in Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, his father, Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont, was 26 and his mother, Julienne de Thourotte, was 26. He had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Léonelle of Salisbury. He died before 1185, in England, and was buried in Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville est né environ 1125, à Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, son père, Nicolas I d’Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Julienne de Thourotte, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles avec Léonelle of Salisbury. Il est décédé avant 1185, à Angleterre, et a été enterré à Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville um 1125 in Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont 26 und seine Mutter Julienne de Thourotte 26. Er hatte mit Léonelle of Salisbury mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb vor 1185 in England und wurde in Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville è nato il circa 1125 nel Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Julienne de Thourotte, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie con Léonelle of Salisbury. Morì prima del 1185, nel England, e fu sepolto a Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuvilleが約1125年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメヴァルモントで生まれた時，父Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmontは26歳，母Julienne de Thourotteは26歳であった。彼はLéonelle of Salisburyとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1185年以前にイギリスで死去し，フランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメヴァルモントにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville(이)가 대략 1125년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 발몽에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Julienne de Thourotte(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Léonelle of Salisbury(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1185년 이전 영국에서 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 발몽에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville nasceu aproximadamente 1125, em Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, seu pai, Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Julienne de Thourotte, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas com Léonelle of Salisbury. Ele faleceu antes 1185, em Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville родился около 1125 г. в местности валмонт, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Julienne de Thourotte, 26. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери от Léonelle of Salisbury. Он умер перед 1185 г. в местности Англия и был похоронен в местности валмонт, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville nació aproximadamente 1125, en Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Nicolas I de Estouteville, Sgr de Valmont, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Julienne de Thourotte, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas con Léonelle of Salisbury. Murió antes de 1185, en Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.
